content: Define the module TopModule with the specified inputs and outputs. This includes clk, load, ena, data, and q.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  ena  (2 bits)
input  data (100 bits)
output q    (100 bits)

content: Implement a 100-bit register to hold the data input. This register should load the data when the load signal is high, otherwise it should hold the previous value. The register updates on the positive edge of clk.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  data (100 bits)

content: Implement the logic to determine the direction of rotation based on the ena signal. Use minimal combinational logic to decide whether to rotate left or right.

Implement the rotation logic that modifies the content of the register based on the direction specified by the ena signal. Ensure that the rotation is done using minimal hardware resources and combinational logic.
source:
input  ena  (2 bits)
input  data (100 bits)

content: Connect the output of the rotation logic to the output port q. This ensures that the rotated data is available as module output.
